defaultMessage: First run of the day. Let's see what the mountain has in store!

positive: No point in sitting down; you're already at the top. Express service!
Reached the peak faster than I could buckle my boots. Warp speed!
No queues? I'll clock more miles today than my car did getting here!
The lifties are working like a Swiss watch today. Hats off!
Pure luxury: straight from the skis to the chair without a pause.
Finally, a resort where I don't spend half the day standing still.
Wow! Before I even tucked my gloves in, I was at the summit!
This is what you pay for - skiing, not loitering at the base.
The lifts are moving faster than my legs can handle today!
Feels like a private mountain. I hope no one else finds out about this!
negative: Spent more time in the queue than on my skis! A total disaster.
My beard has grown an inch since we started waiting for this Girak.
Is there free goulash at the top? Why is everyone waiting here?
Management, are you asleep? The line stretches to the next village!
Three more songs on the radio and we might actually reach the ramp.
This isn't a ski resort; it's an exercise in patience in the cold.
If I wanted to stand in line, I'd go to the bank, not the mountain.
The coffee in my thermos went cold and we're still not at the front.
This Poma is moving like a snail on anti-inflammatory cream.
Next time I'm bringing a chair and a chessboard for this queue.

positive: Finally, room to carve a turn so sharp even the legends would cheer!
Peace and quiet... just me, my thoughts, and the fresh powder.
No slalom around people! Skiing like a king on his private estate.
The slope is so empty I could practically ski with my eyes closed.
This is the life - freedom on the mountain without fear of collision.
Proper space for high-speed turns without hitting the brakes.
The mountain is all mine today. Heaven on earth!
Skiing an empty run is like dancing in an empty ballroom.
No beginners cutting me off today. Absolute perfection!
Finally, a place where you can actually relax and enjoy the speed.
negative: This isn't a slope; it's a highway at rush hour! Dangerous.
Someone nearly flattened me! There's no room left to even turn.
Everyone is out today - from infants to great-grandmothers. A total anthill!
Watch your head! People are flying past like unguided rockets.
Crowdier here than the town market on a Saturday morning.
Skiing in this mob feels more like survival than a sport.
I have to stop every ten meters just to avoid a pile-up.
This run has become a collision course. I'm heading home!
Did they only open one run for a thousand people? Catastrophic crowds.
There's more legroom on the city bus than on this slope.

positive: These corduroy ridges are so perfect I want to frame them!
Snow like butter - the skis turn themselves. Kudos to the cat crew!
First-class powder. Even the clouds are jealous of this surface!
The edges hold like they're glued. The groomers did a masterclass.
Morning skiing on these fresh tracks was pure poetry.
The run is as flat as if it were pressed with a hot iron. Pure joy!
Even this afternoon, the snow is holding its shape. Brilliant base!
No ice, no moguls, just pure bliss on the snow.
Hats off to whoever was driving the Ratrac last night. A master!
Best grooming of the season. No debate.
negative: Am I skiing or ice-skating? The slope is hard as concrete!
These moguls are going to ruin my knees. Like skiing on a washing machine.
I made one turn and ended up in the woods. The snow is appalling.
I need skates here, not skis. Nothing but sheets of ice!
The run looks like a battlefield after a bombardment. Pits and piles.
Did they sell the groomers or are they all broken? This snow is a disaster.
Hard to enjoy yourself when you're fighting bumps as high as a house.
My skis are vibrating more than an old spin-cycle on a washer.
Pure ice. Management is clearly cutting corners on grooming.
A run like this only brings sore legs and zero pleasure.
